verify.cooldown: One sec - wait a moment, then tap **Verify** again.
verify.instructions: Type the characters shown below (not case-sensitive). New image each try.
verify.expired: That code expired - tap **Verify** for a fresh one.
verify.roleMissing: Passed - but the verified role is missing. Ping a mod.
verify.tooMany: Too many wrong tries - tap **Verify** for a new image.
verify.wrong: That's not right - tap **Verify** for a new image and try again. ({left} left)
verify.panelText:
**Verify & Agree to the Rules**
Click **Verify** and type the code from the image. Verifying confirms you’ve read and agree to the rules above, and unlocks the server.

appeal.invalid: That appeal link is no longer valid - you may already have been unbanned, or already sent an appeal for this ban.
appeal.busy: The mod team's inbox is busy right now - please try again in a minute.
appeal.sent: ✅ Your appeal was sent to **{guild}**'s mod team. If they approve it, I'll DM you a fresh invite.
appeal.unreachable: Sorry - I couldn't reach that server's mod team ({error}). Try again in a moment.
appeal.dmIntro: ⚠️ You were banned by a **MadHoney** honeypot - a hidden channel that exists only to catch spam bots. Anything posted there is banned automatically.
appeal.dmAsk: If you're a real person who ended up in one by mistake, you can ask **{guild}**'s mod team to take another look:
appeal.approvedInvite: ✅ Your appeal to **{guild}** was approved. Rejoin here: {invite}
appeal.approvedNoInvite: ✅ Your appeal to **{guild}** was approved. Ask a member for a fresh invite to rejoin.
appeal.denied: Your appeal to **{guild}** was reviewed and not approved.

reply.needManageServer: You need **Manage Server** to use MadHoney controls.
reply.noHoneypotYet: No honeypot channel set yet - run `/madhoney setup` first.
reply.banshareOn: 🌐 **Ban sharing ON** - users on the universal MadHoney ban list (caught by any server's honeypot) are auto-banned when they join here. Run `/madhoney bansync` to also ban everyone already on the list.
reply.banshareOff: 🔒 **Isolated** - this server acts only on its own honeypot catches. (Your catches still feed the universal list for servers that opt in.)
reply.modeArmed: 🍯 **Honeypot armed.** Accounts that post in the honeypot are banned immediately.
reply.modeReview: ⏸ **Honeypot set to hold for review.** Honeypot posts are reported to your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons instead of an instant ban - a mod decides. Not recommended for busy servers, and it needs a log channel set.
reply.modeDisarmed: ⭘ **Honeypot disarmed.** The trap is off - nobody gets banned until you arm it again.
reply.modeReviewNoLog: ⚠️ You have no log channel set, so held posts have nowhere to go. Set one under **Staff & log**.

log.reportBanned: 🍯 **The following message was posted in the honeypot** (<#{channel}>) **and the user has been banned.**
log.reportFailed: 🍯 **The following message was posted in the honeypot** (<#{channel}>) **and the user has been ⚠️ NOT banned (ban failed - check my permissions).**
log.reviewHeld: ⏸ **Held for review.** {tag} (`{id}`) posted in the honeypot (<#{channel}>). **Nobody has been banned yet.**
log.banshareReport: 🌐 **Auto-banned on join** - this user is on the universal MadHoney ban list (caught by another server's honeypot).
log.webhookAlert: 🪝 **A webhook posted in the honeypot** (<#{channel}>) and its message was deleted. Webhooks aren't members, so they can't be banned - open **Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks** and delete any you don't recognize.
log.appealForward: 📩 **Ban appeal** - <@{id}> ({tag}, `{id}`) was banned by the honeypot and is asking you to take another look.
log.unbannedBy: ✅ **Unbanned** by {mod} - they can rejoin (send them a fresh invite; ban-share won't re-ban them).

setup.title: ## MadHoney setup
setup.dashHint: 💡 **The web dashboard is the easy way to do this.** {dash} is far more powerful and user-friendly than these chat commands - drag-and-drop channel gating, a live banner designer, appeals, and clearer guidance. These commands work too, if you prefer Discord.
setup.verifiedRole: **Verified role:** {role} - pick an existing role, or create one first (Server Settings → Roles, e.g. "Verified").
setup.verifyChannel: **Verify channel:** {channel} - where the Verify button lives. Your **#rules** channel is the classic spot.
setup.honeypotChannel: **Honeypot channel:** {channel} - create a decoy channel bots will post in. Name it like a real channel: `general-2`, `general2`, `chat-2`. Anyone who posts there is banned.
setup.staffRole: **Staff role (optional):** {role} - members with it are never trapped (admins with **Manage Server** and the owner are always exempt). Set it under **Staff & log**.
setup.logChannel: **Log channel (optional):** {channel} - every honeypot ban is reported there with an **Unban** button. Set it under **Staff & log**.
setup.hpReview: ⏸ **Hold for review** - a mod approves each hit
setup.onboarding: ⚠️ If you use Discord **Onboarding**, make sure it does NOT auto-grant the verified role, or the captcha is bypassable.
setup.accessibility: ♿ Honeypots are visual traps - not recommended for servers serving visually impaired communities. At minimum, name the honeypot in your rules so text-to-speech users hear the warning.
setup.whenReady: When all three are set, run **/madhoney deploy**.
setup.clash: ❌ Verify and honeypot must be **different** channels.

more.title: ## Staff & log
more.staffRole: **Staff role:** {role} - anyone with this role can post in the honeypot without being banned. The owner and anyone with **Manage Server** are always exempt; set this for mods who don't have that permission.
more.logChannel: **Log channel:** {channel} - a staff-only channel; every honeypot ban is reported there with an **Unban** button in case someone trips it by accident.

deploy.title: ## Deploy MadHoney
deploy.dashHint: 💡 Prefer buttons and a live preview? Do all of this on the dashboard: {dash}
deploy.step1: 1. **Grandfather members** - give everyone already here the verified role
deploy.step2: 2. **Post Verify panel** - the button + captcha, in your verify channel
deploy.step3: 3. **Post honeypot banner** - the warning image (design it with `/madhoney banner`)

banner.preview: Banner preview - saved. Tweak with `/madhoney banner`, or post it:

stats.title: MadHoney - Statistics
stats.subtitle: Live numbers across every server running MadHoney.
stats.chartAria: Unique spammers caught per day over the last 30 days
stats.chartNote: Each spammer is counted once, on the day they were first caught - a bot banned in several servers is one spammer, not many.
stats.onlyYou: Only shown to you - the servers you own, have Manage Server in, or hold a staff/admin role in.

guild.refreshTitle: Re-fetch roles, channels and members from Discord
guild.armDescArmed: Anyone who posts in the honeypot is banned immediately.
guild.armDescReview: Honeypot posts are held and reported to your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons - a mod decides. Not recommended for busy servers.
guild.armDescDisarmed: The trap is off - nobody gets banned. Arm it once everything is set up.
guild.armReviewNoLog: Set a log channel below - held posts have nowhere to go otherwise.
guild.guideP1: Why MadHoney works this way. A spam bot never posts once - it blasts every channel it can reach and pings @everyone if it can, and every message leaves that channel marked unread. Banning the bot and deleting the spam doesn't clear those unread markers, so you're left with a whole server flagged for spam nobody should have seen. MadHoney catches the bot in one place before it can touch the rest of your server.
guild.guideP2: Bots aren't smart, and the design leans on that: gate every channel behind the verified role and the only place an unverified account can post is the honeypot. Name it like a real channel (general-2) and an indiscriminate bot walks straight in. Its only content is an image - a human reads the warning and backs off, but a bot can't parse a picture, so it posts anyway and trips the trap. The verified role hides the honeypot from anyone who passed the captcha, so no real member ever gets caught. That's why verification is the recommended setup - it's what keeps humans out of the trap.
guild.guideLi1: Verified role first. Create a role (e.g. "Verified") in Server Settings → Roles, then drag the MadHoney role ABOVE it. If MadHoney's role sits below the verified role, gating and grandfathering fail with a permission error - this is the single most common mistake.
guild.guideLi2: Configure below. Pick the verified role, your verify channel (usually #rules), and a honeypot channel. Optionally add a staff role and a log channel.
guild.guideLi3: Grandfather members. Run this first so everyone already in the server gets the verified role and nobody is locked out when you gate.
guild.guideLi4: Post the Verify panel and honeypot banner.
guild.guideLi5: Gate channels. Open the drag board, review what MadHoney detected, move anything it misjudged, then apply. Nothing changes until you hit Apply.
guild.tipNaming: 🍯 Naming the honeypot: name it like a real channel so bots post in it. Good: general-2, chat-2, off-topic-2. Bad: honeypot, do-not-post (some spam tools skip those).
guild.tipMode: 🔘 Honeypot mode: the control at the top has three settings. Armed bans on sight (recommended). Off disables the trap while you set up. Review holds each hit in your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons so a mod decides - not recommended for busy servers (a real spam run floods the log), but it works if you want a human in the loop.
guild.tipOnboarding: ⚠️ Discord Onboarding: if you use it, make sure it does NOT auto-grant the verified role, or the captcha can be skipped.
guild.tipPerm: 🔒 Getting a permission error? It's almost always the MadHoney role sitting below your verified role, or missing Manage Roles / Manage Channels. Re-invite MadHoney with ＋ Add server in the top bar, then drag its role to the top of your staff roles.
guild.verifiedRoleHint: Granted after the captcha. MadHoney's own role must sit ABOVE it.
guild.verifyChannelHint: Where the Verify button lives - your #rules channel is the classic spot.
guild.honeypotChannelHint: The trap. Name it like a real channel (general-2). Posting here = instant ban.
guild.logChannelHint: Staff-only channel - each ban is reported there with an Unban button.
guild.deleteHint: How much of a trapped user's recent message history to wipe when banned. Discord's max is 7 days.
guild.staffRoleHint: Never trapped by the honeypot, and can manage MadHoney here.
guild.adminRoleHint: Dashboard access WITHOUT the honeypot exemption - for helpers.
guild.requireVerifyHint: This is the whole point of MadHoney. It gates your channels behind the verified role so the honeypot is the only place an unverified account can post, and it hides the honeypot from real members so no human ever gets caught. Turn this off and the honeypot still bans, but it's visible to everyone, so a member could wander in and get banned - you'd be relying only on the warning banner.
guild.verifOffWarn: ⚠️ Verification is OFF. The honeypot is not hidden from your members, so a human could post in it and get banned. Only the warning banner protects them. We strongly recommend turning verification back on.
guild.captchaHint: Harder = longer code and more OCR-defeating distortion. Raise it if bots start solving your captcha; lower it if real members struggle.
guild.botLanguageHint: The language MadHoney speaks in this server - the verify panel, captcha replies, and appeal DMs. (This dashboard follows your own browser language, which is separate.)
guild.verifyMessageHint: Shown above the Verify button. Leave blank to use the default in your bot language.
guild.creditNote: ℹ️ A small "protected by MadHoney" credit line is shown on your verify panel (not the honeypot banner). Want it gone? MadHoney is free and open - self-host it (SELF_HOSTED=true) and you can remove it.
guild.applyList: Apply the universal ban list to this server
guild.applyListHint: Every honeypot catch across all MadHoney servers feeds one list. ON: users on it are banned when they join here (use "Ban from List" below to apply it retroactively). OFF: this server acts only on its own catches - which it keeps either way.
guild.letAppeal: Let banned users appeal by DM
guild.letAppealHint: When a honeypot ban happens, MadHoney DMs the user offering to appeal. If they do, the request lands in your log channel with Approve (unban + fresh invite) / Deny buttons. A user only ever sees servers they were actually banned from that turned this on - never anything else.
guild.bannerNote:
Live preview - it re-renders as you tweak. Save, then post it from Actions below.
Make it yours. If every MadHoney honeypot looked identical, bots could learn to recognize and skip it. Change the text, colors, font or logo so your banner is one of a kind. (The file is also posted under a random name each time, for the same reason.)
guild.headline: Headline
guild.mentionHighlightHint: Anything written as #channel or @role gets a pill in this color.
guild.roleColoringHint: "Real role colors" pulls each @role's color from Discord; #channels and colorless roles use the custom color.
guild.logoHint: Direct PNG/JPG URL for your own logo, or type none for no logo.
guild.distortionHint: Garbles the text captcha-style so OCR bots can't read the warning and skip the trap. Higher = harder for machines (and slightly harder for humans). Preview it above before you post.
guild.hideCredit: Hide the "protected by MadHoney" credit line
guild.hideCreditHint: You're self-hosting, so this is your call. The credit shows as a small link on your verify panel (not on the honeypot banner, so the decoy stays generic).
guild.deployHeader: Deploy — run 1 → 4 in order on first setup
guild.step1Desc: Gives the verified role to every human already in the server, so gating never locks anyone out. Bots and already-verified members are skipped.
guild.step2Desc: Posts the Verify button in the verify channel. New members click it, read a captcha, and receive the verified role.
guild.step3Desc: Posts the warning image (designed above) into the honeypot channel. Re-run after changing the banner.
guild.step4Desc: Opens the channel picker: MadHoney classifies every channel (public / private / admin) and you choose exactly which to hide behind the verified role. Nothing changes until you apply.
guild.ungateDesc: Reverses gating: returns the channels MadHoney gated to how they looked before. Admin channels it never touched are left alone.
guild.banSyncDesc: Bans everyone on the universal ban list now, instead of waiting for them to join. Needs the universal list turned ON above.
guild.noBans: No bans logged in this server yet.

msg.bannerSaved: Banner saved. Post it from Actions (or /madhoney deploy in Discord).
msg.channelClash: ❌ Verify and honeypot must be different channels - not saved.
msg.noteArmed: 🍯 Honeypot armed. Anyone who posts in the honeypot channel is now banned.
msg.noteReview: ⏸ Honeypot set to Hold for review. Honeypot posts go to your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons instead of an instant ban.
msg.noteReviewNoLog: ⚠️ Set a log channel below so held posts have somewhere to go.
msg.noteDisarmed: ⭘ Honeypot disarmed. The trap is off - nobody gets banned until you arm it again.
msg.jobRunning: A job is already running - watch the bar below.
msg.refreshed: Refreshed roles and channels from Discord.
